findings.summary: TT5L side-info usefulness remains unproven at full trained shape.
findings.details: The full 600-pair recovered TT5L packet has 0/27000 nonzero side-info values. Current code can emit nonzero side-info in a 2-pair macOS-CPU advisory smoke, but no full 600-pair trained archive proves useful nonzero side-info yet.

findings.summary: Tracked custody artifacts lag current no-op hardening.
findings.details: Current tt5l_sideinfo_variant_packets.py records source-to-variant archive/member/side-section change booleans, but tracked variant packet JSONs lack those fields. Durable .omx/research artifacts should be regenerated before using them for dispatch or architecture-lock evidence.

findings.summary: Provider and axis schemas are easy to misfeed.
findings.details: Result review packets use score_axis while effect-curve cells require axis plus variant. Seed-cell wrappers normalize one path, but direct reviewed-result ingestion can still produce missing-axis or missing-variant failures. Pair identity must remain validator-enforced rather than inferred.

The architecture-lock packet still has architecture_lock_allowed=false. Blockers include missing valid side-info effect curve, missing first-anchor timing smoke, missing paired-axis/probe evidence, Modal billing-limit blocker, and Lightning alternate-provider prerequisites.

recommended_next_actions: Produce a full 600-pair current-code TT5L trained archive with nonzero side-info.
Rebuild zero, random_lsb, shuffled, trained, and ablated variants from that source.
Run claimed paired contest-CPU and contest-CUDA exact eval for all five cells.

Regenerate tracked variant, effect-curve, and materialized-work-unit artifacts from current code so .omx/research carries the hardened byte-change/no-op evidence before any new dispatch or architecture-lock refresh.
